rationale: The citing paper uses Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely (2005) in the theoretical development of its free-versus-paid boundary condition (H3), not just as a generic background citation. It specifically cites that work to support the claim that paid products create consumer expectancies about benefits, which is then used to justify why pricing should shape learning mindset and appeal effectiveness. Although the paper does not reuse the placebo-performance design or test Shiv et al.'s mechanism directly, the cited finding meaningfully informs the citing paper's conceptual setup for a tested hypothesis.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Shiv et al. (2005)'s expectancy-based pricing logic to education marketing. It uses that prior evidence to motivate and test its prediction that paid versus free education contexts alter how consumers respond to cognitive versus affective appeals.
